Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

A patch touching this many files is bound to have many conflicts and/or break in-flight patches.

At a minimum, the ptlrpc_req_finished() -> ptlrpc_req_put() renaming should be added to spelling.txt to warn people about this, since it is used fairly often.  Luckily, the Gerrit checkpatch spelling.txt is being updated from master daily, so it should be picked up pretty quickly *after* this patch is landed.

It seems reasonable to add a short-lived compat macro to allow in-flight patches to not break outright, then remove it in a later patch:

 #if LUSTRE_VERSION_CODE < OBD_OCD_VERSION(2, 15, 57)
 #define ptlrpc_req_finished(rq) ptlrpc_req_done(rq)
 #endif

possibly the patch to remove even the conditional code could be landed in a couple of months.

Other than the staging issues, I definitely agree with the name change.

Luckily, the Gerrit checkpatch spelling.txt is being updated from master daily, so it should be picked up pretty quickly *after* this patch is landed.
> 
> It seems reasonable to add a short-lived compat macro to allow in-flight patches to not break outright, then remove it in a later patch:
> 
>  #if LUSTRE_VERSION_CODE < OBD_OCD_VERSION(2, 15, 57)
>  #define ptlrpc_req_finished(rq) ptlrpc_req_done(rq)
>  #endif
> 
> possibly the patch to remove even the conditional code could be landed in a couple of months.
> 
> Other than the staging issues, I definitely agree with the name change.

done and done on spelling.txt and a compat macro; I may wait to push until you've reviewed some of the others since it's going to rebase the whole series

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Other than the spelling and compat macro, seems straight forward.

Patch Set 10: Code-Review-1

(5 comments)

Arshad, thanks for refreshing this.  It would be good to chip away at the patches in this series, but just one at a time so there isn't too much churn.  

Some minor style issues if patch is refreshed.  I think the version check is a deal breaker for me, since we are at 2.15.60 now and it might be a week before this patch is landed (if nothing else conflicts first), and by then Oleg might tag 2.15.61 and the build and/or other inflight patches would break.

Given how widely used this function is, I'd like to give it a bit more time to settle in, and have the checkpatch warning appear for at least a few weeks so we avoid old patches landing that didn't get any warning.

commit.subject: LU-16741 ptlrpc: rename ptlrpc_req_finished
commit.message:
LU-16741 ptlrpc: rename ptlrpc_req_finished

ptlrpc_req_finished naming is odd - it actually just 'puts'
the request.  Yes, it's called when a request is complete,
but the name is still (imo) misleading.

Change it as part of a general refactor of the ptlrpc
request put/freeing code.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I43a9afd1ddd05166730d680b6aedaefadc9da495
